decisions.briefing: Markets posted modest gains today (S&P +0.6%, NASDAQ +0.4%) with a neutral tone — no strong directional catalyst. With only $226.69 in cash (below the $500 threshold), no new positions can be opened meaningfully. Current holdings include NOK which scores 95 on the screener, confirming it remains a solid hold. All six positions are retained as the portfolio sits at a strong +29.6% overall.

market_education.summary_en: Equities drifted modestly higher on June 12 as investors maintained a cautious but constructive stance heading into the summer. Technology names led gains on the NASDAQ, supported by continued strength in AI-related semiconductor stocks. Broader participation across sectors kept S&P 500 gains steady, though low conviction and thin volume suggested traders were holding positions rather than initiating new ones.
market_education.summary_zh: 6月12日美股溫和收漲，投資者在進入夏季前保持謹慎但偏多的態度。納斯達克由AI相關半導體股領漲，科技板塊表現突出。標普500在各板塊普遍小幅回升下穩步上揚，但成交量清淡、買盤力道不足，顯示市場參與者以守代攻，傾向持股觀望而非積極建倉。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio. While cash provides safety and flexibility, it earns near-zero returns, which can reduce overall portfolio performance when markets are rising. Today's situation — only $226.69 available — illustrates why keeping a small but meaningful cash reserve (ideally $500+) matters for being able to act on new opportunities.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」（Cash Drag）是指投資組合中持有閒置現金所帶來的績效損耗。現金雖提供安全性與靈活度，但在市場上漲期間，幾乎零回報的現金部位會拖低整體投資組合的表現。今日情況正是一例——手頭僅剩226.69美元，低於500美元的最低操作門檻，無法把握市場出現的新進場機會，凸顯保留適當規模備用現金的重要性。
